channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: New Zealand vow to silence Indian crowd as India seek to defend T20 World Cup title in Ahmedabad final
item.description: New Zealand captain Mitchell Santner has said his team are ready to silence the massive Indian crowd when they face India in the ICC Men’s T20 World Cup final at the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad...

item.title: Orange Sky marks a decade supporting Canberra residents with mobile laundry service
item.description: Orange Sky has been running a mobile laundry service in Canberra for the past ten years, offering not just clean clothes but also human connection for people experiencing homelessness or hardship. Vol...

item.title: Wrexham and Chelsea Draw in FA Cup Fifth Round
item.description: Wrexham hosted Chelsea in the FA Cup fifth round, producing an exciting match at the historic Racecourse Ground. The Welsh side, under the ownership of Ryan Reynolds and Rob McElhenney, have made a re...

item.title: Profile of Araireoluwa Oladire: Nigerian Artist Merging Art, Design, and Music Culture
item.description: Araireoluwa Oladire na visual artist and interior designer wey dey Ibadan, Oyo State, Nigeria. Him work dey focused on how identity, culture, fashion, and social architecture dey intersect. Oladire de...

item.title: The contrasting deaths of Ian von Memerty and Soli Philander spark national reflection
item.description: This week, South Africa witnessed two high-profile deaths in the entertainment industry that drew very different public reactions. Ian von Memerty, 61, a judge on SA's Got Talent, took his own life in...

item.title: NHRC Chief Calls for Revamp of Paris Principles to Strengthen Global Human Rights Standards
item.description: During the Raisina Dialogue in New Delhi, NHRC Chairperson Justice V Ramasubramanian stressed the urgent need to revamp the Paris Principles in light of ongoing global conflicts. He highlighted that t...

item.title: Iran's President Offers Conditional Halt to Attacks on Neighbours Amid Escalating US-Israel Conflict
item.description: The ongoing US-Israel war against Iran, now in its second week and codenamed Operation Epic Fury, continues to escalate with significant regional repercussions. Iran's President Masoud Pezeshkian issu...

item.title: Navi Mumbai Students Demonstrate Innovative STEM Projects at Science Carnival
item.description: The seventh edition of the Amazon Think Big Science Carnival was organised at the CIDCO Exhibition Centre in Vashi, Navi Mumbai, on March 6, 2026. This initiative, supported by Amazon and implemented ...

item.title: Omah Lay Sets April 3 for Release of Second Album ‘Clarity of Mind'
item.description: After long wait wey fans don dey anticipate, Omah Lay don finally announce say him second album 'Clarity of Mind' go drop on April 3, 2026. The Afrobeats star share the news for social media after tea...

item.title: Three Chinese Nationals Arrested at Clark Airport for Carrying P70 Million Marijuana
item.description: Three Chinese nationals were apprehended at Clark International Airport in Pampanga for allegedly carrying high-grade marijuana, locally called 'kush,' valued at P70 million. The Philippine Drug Enfor...

item.title: Heavy Rainfall and Potential Flooding Alert Issued for Ontario
item.description: Environment Canada has issued a special weather statement for parts of Ontario, warning of significant rainfall, dense fog, and a heightened risk of flooding. Between 15 and 40 millimetres of rain are...

item.title: Floods in Kenya Kill 25 Amid Criticism of Slow Government Response
item.description: Heavy rains have battered Kenya, especially Nairobi, leading to devastating floods that have claimed 25 lives. The floods have caused massive destruction, submerging vehicles, damaging over 100 cars, ...

item.title: Kaizer Chiefs fans submit formal complaints to management after poor run of results
item.description: On Saturday morning, a small group of Kaizer Chiefs supporters gathered outside the club’s Naturena headquarters to express their dissatisfaction with the team’s recent performances. The club has lost...

item.title: Australia’s cautious alignment with US actions on Iran raises concerns over international law
item.description: The article examines the potential risks of Australia aligning too closely with the United States amid escalating tensions with Iran. Zoe Daniel highlights concerns about Prime Minister Anthony Albane...

item.title: Lagos State Government Issues Warning to Actress Sarah Martins Over Street Cooking for Charity
item.description: The Lagos State Government don warn Nollywood actress Sarah Martins say make she stop to cook and share food for public roads inside the state. Commissioner for Environment and Water Resources, Tokunb...

item.title: UK Government to Operate Charter Flight from Dubai for British Nationals Amid Regional Conflict
item.description: The UK government has announced plans to operate a charter flight from Dubai early next week to assist British nationals wishing to leave the region, depending on the local security situation. The fli...

item.title: James Orengo Calls for Official Inquiry Into Raila Odinga's Passing
item.description: On Sunday, March 8, Kenyan newspapers reported extensively on multiple issues, notably the severe flooding in Nairobi, which prompted President William Ruto to intervene. Besides the natural disaster,...

item.title: MOI Implements Expansion Projects to Reduce Surgery Waiting Times
item.description: Dodoma – Muhimbili Orthopaedic Institute (MOI) in Dar es Salaam explain say long waiting times for surgery are mostly because of limited theatre space and big increase in trauma cases. MOI Executive D...

item.title: Julian Barnes Concludes His Literary Journey with ‘Departure(s)’
item.description: Julian Barnes, the 2011 Booker Prize-winning author, has released his fifteenth and final novel, 'Departure(s)', marking the end of a remarkable literary career. At 80 years old, Barnes presents a nov...

item.title: Hennie du Plessis Leads Joburg Open After Marathon 27-Hole Effort
item.description: South Africa’s Hennie du Plessis has taken the lead at the Joburg Open after a demanding day on the course, completing a total of 27 holes on Saturday. Du Plessis had to finish the suspended second ro...
